item.title: Ysgol Llanilar — Cyanotype accordion activity guide
item.description:
Outline

  1. Overview
  2. Session 1: Making cyanotypes
  3. Session 2: Making the accordion book
  4. Reflection and Discussion
  5. Expected outcome

Duration

Years 5–6 (Ages 9–11)

  • Session 1: Cyanotype Printing (90–120 minutes)
  • Session 2: Accordion Book Construction (60–90 minutes)

1. Overview

Pupils will explore the historic photographic process of cyanotype printing using natural materials collected from their local environment. They will then transform their cyanotype prints into a handmade seven-page accordion book.

History of cyanotype

The cyanotype process was invented in 1842 by Sir John Herschel. It is one of the earliest photographic printing techniques and produces distinctive white images on a deep blue background.

item.title: Ysgol Aberteifi — Magical Wings activity guide
item.description:
Magical Wings - creating layered artworks with artist Pip Lewis

This guide describes the processes involved in creating layered artworks using acetate and paper, drawing and marker art techniques. 

The art process were designed to explore and learn about the range of pattern, line and colour found on the wings of specific Butterflies and Moths species living in Oak Tree  - and the function this has on their survival.

Index

  1. Pattern sheet
  2. Photo lottery
  3. Tracing outline
  4. Adding patterns
  5. Adding detail
  6. Naming the species

1. Pattern sheet 

In the first exercise the pupils made a pattern sheet , with seven of the most common patterns and shapes found on butterfly and moth’s wings.

item.title: Ysgol Llanarth Workshop 5 — Gilding & poetry
item.description:
Words to follow!

Oriel Gallery

Clay making

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi Workshop 5 — Moths & Butterflies of the Oak
item.description:
In the final workshop, Group B students added the colour detail of the patterns to the butterfly and moth wings, following on from the tracing and outlining in workshop 4. They also added the Welsh translation of their English names.

The students had an evaluation at the end of the morning, based on three questions:

  1. How much did you learn?
  2. How proud of yourself are you?
  3. Which part of the artwork did you enjoy the most?

The students added their response to each question by adding a mark – the nearer the centre the mark was placed on the target indicated the higher the student rated their achievement /enjoyment or learning relating to the specific questions.

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron Workshop 5 — Character creation
Ysgol Llanilar Workshop 5 — Cyanotope covers & ribbon
item.description:
Post by Jake Rayson

It was a very chill morning session with not too much to do. Marian showed the students how to create the end covers out of card and patch of cyanotype, then measure up some ribbon and glue it all together.

After the students had finished all their cutting and their gluing and their assembling, we were going to go out for a final cyanotype session, but the summer rain had different ideas.

item.title: Ysgol Llanarth Workshop 4 — Painting & clay artefact creation
item.description:
Sketch by Jake Rayson

The weather was magnificent but I was feeling under the weather. Fortunately, the students kept me alert with a thousand questions on the walk to Cae Ysgubor, AKA Molly’s Field. I was bringing up the rear and was continually distracted by plants and wildlife.

By the time we reached the quiet country lane, a bountiful harvest of Sticky Willy / Llau’r Offeiriad (Galium aparine) was to be made, which we graciously donated to Molly’s horses. They love the stuff.

item.title: Moth & butterfly species and the Oak / Derwen
item.description:
For the second set of workshops, artist Pip Lewis prepared a whole set of colour print outs of moths and butterflies that are associated with the Oak tree. Here is the list of their names in Latin, English and Welsh, plus links to Wikipedia pages.

Butterfly and Moth species

Moth species English name Welsh name
Comibaena bajularia Blotched Emerald Emrallt Blotiog
Lymantria monacha Black Arches Siobyn Bwaog
Carterocephalus palaemon Chequered Skipper Gwibiwr Y Llennyrch
Catocala sponsa Dark Crimson Underwing Ôl-adain Ruddgoch Dywyll
Apoda limacodes Festoon Llusgwyfyn Rhuban
Polyploca ridens Frosted Green Tant Siriol
Malacosoma neustria Lackey Gwaswyfyn
Acleris literana Lichen Button -
Cyclophora punctaria Maiden’s Blush Moca Gwridog
Griposia aprilina Merveille du Jour Gwyfyn Cennog
Watsonalla binaria Oak Hook-tip Bachadain Y Deri
Cymatophorina diluta Oak Lutestring Tant Y Deri
Biston strataria Oak Beauty Rhisglyn Y Derw
Lasiocampa quercus Oak Eggar Wylun Deri
Crocallis elinguaria Scalloped Oak Carpiog Y Derw
Plagodis dolabraria Scorched Wing Adain Ddeifiog
Butterfly species English name Welsh name
Apatura iris Purple Emperor Mantell Borffor
Pararge aegeria Speckled Wood Gweirlöyn Brych

item.title: Ysgol Llanilar Workshop 4 — Cyanotype collage strips
item.description: Marian showed the students examples of the cyanotype collage strips that they’ll be creating, and then handed out the students‘ negative acetate prints in envelopes. These will form the textured background on the cyanotype. And then we went outside to gather plant material.

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi Workshop 4 — Moths & Butterflies of the Oak
item.description:
Guest post by artist Pip Lewis

Group B – Butterflies & Moths!

Patterns in Nature - Introduction 

Pip introduced the theme of Butterflies and Moths living in oak Trees and the patterns and shapes they have on their wings and showed an example of the layered artworks of butterflies and moths that live and forage in Oak Trees which the pupils will be making.

The art process involved in making the final image will include tracing, working with colour, black and white and marker pens,  patterns shapes and letter stencils as well as incorporating design and layout skills for the final text.

item.title: Habitat guidelines — where to put up nest boxes
item.description:
We ran a workshop with Harry Smalley from Cardished at Ysgol Llanilar, constructing kit nest boxes. We then walked around the school grounds and village pond, looking for potential sites.

Habitat overview

Mosaic of habitat – pond, river, streams, woodland, marsh, grassland, hedges, gardens, houses, buildings…

Different habitats from satellite photo

Siting habitat

Bat boxes

Bat Conservation Trust

  1. Where bats are known to feed and navigate (close to hedges and tree lines);
  2. Ideally at least 4m above the ground (where safe installation is possible);
  3. Away from artificial light sources (to protect them from predation);
  4. Sheltered from strong winds; and
  5. Exposed to the sun for part of the day (usually south, south-east or south-west).
  6. North Ceredigion Bat Group northceredigionbatgroup@gmail.com & facebook.com/groups/northceredigionbatgroup

Nest boxes

British Trust for Ornithology

item.title: Ysgol Llanilar — Habitat workshop with Cardished
item.description:
Harry Smalley from Cardished ran a fantastic habitat workshop with Year 5 students from Llanilar. He brought two finished Barbastrelle bat boxes, four nest box apartments for House Sparrows and kits for Robin and Starling sizes nest boxes that the students assembled, using a form, a clamp, hammers and nails.

After the workshop, we walked around the grounds to look at possible sites for the nestboxes, and then we had a quick walk around the village pond to talk about habitat and the importance of water and flying insects for bats.

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi — Oak leaf collages
item.description:
Inspired by artist Pip Lewis‘s most excellent collage exercise with the bird artwork the previous week with Group A, teacher Mari Roberts created collages of Oak leaves with Group B based on observational drawings the children had made.

This is an excellent example of how techniques, skills and knowledge can be transferred across different groups within the school.

Oriel Gallery

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron Workshop 4 — Exquisite Corpses
item.description:
Rebecca took full charge of the workshop, creating creatures from all the plants, fungi and animals of the wood and countryside surrounding Tŷ Mari.

More information to follow!

Oriel Gallery

item.title: Trefnu / Organise
item.description:
People

School Naturalist Creative Teacher
Aberteifi Yusef Samari Pip Lewis Mari Roberts
Aberaeron Carys May Rebecca Wyn Kelly Carys Griffiths-Jones
Llanarth Jo Dainty Molly Gilbert Cally Mason
Llanilar Sheena Duller Marian Haf Jan Jones

Aberteifi organise page: storiwyllt.cymru/organise-aberteifi

  • Emrys Plant - Creative Custodian
  • Sheena Duller - Ecology Advisor
  • Jake Rayson - Facilitator

Celebration event

  • Mon 29 Jun - 6pm-8pm
  • Stiwdio 3, Aberteifi
  • Work on display until Mon 6 Jul
  • School visits Wed 8, Thu 9, Fri 10 Jul

Alphabetical people

  • Emrys Plant - Creative Custodian
  • Cally Mason - Teacher, Llanarth
  • Carys Griffiths-Jones - Teacher, Aberaeron
  • Carys May - Ecologist, Aberaeron
  • Jake Rayson - Facilitator
  • Jannette Jones - Teacher, Llanilar
  • Jo Dainty - Forest school practitioner, Llanarth
  • Marian Haf - Artist, Llanilar
  • Mari Roberts - Teacher, Aberteifi
  • Molly Gilbert - Artist, Llanarth
  • Pip Lewis - Artist, Aberteifi
  • Rebecca Wyn Kelly - Artist, Aberaeron
  • Sheena Duller - Ecologist, Llanilar & Ecology Advisor
  • Yusef Samari - Ecologist, Aberteifi

Summary

Combining wildlife ID with habitat creation and storytelling, culminating in bilingual wild stories celebration in Aberteifi in late June.

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron Workshop 3 — The magic of Tŷ Mari
item.description:
We finally managed to go to the house of Mari Berllan Biter on the Esgair Arth estate.

The minibus arrived filled with students and staff, and we gathered around the entrance to the field, with the song of the Willow Warbler, identified by ecologist Carys May.

We set off across the tussocky pasture, diagonally left toward the corner, where the footpath descends down through the wood. Along the way, we saw voles plenty of spiders, mole hills and evidence of voles.

item.title: Ysgol Llanarth Workshop 3 — Flower prints & painting
item.description:
Guest post by our forest school practitioner Jo Dainty

On the walk up Joey collected a posey of flowers which was so lovely. There was lots of chatter and excitement.  Alfie set the pace and it was a bit of a route march to keep up with him! 

We started by introducing Molly’s sister (author of the nature story that has been shared with the class) to the group and enjoyed lots of chat about the characters!  We had the circle of mats again – really lovely for everyone to feel like an equal part of the group.

item.title: List of species at Esgair Arth
item.description:
Ecologist Carys May from WWBIC did an amazing job finding, explaining, photographing and cataloguing a whole load of different species on Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron‘s trip to Tŷ Mari at Esgair Arth.


Swallow landing on a telephone wire, photo by Carys May

Species English name Welsh name Species group
Acer pseudoplatanus Sycamore Masarnen Flowering plant
Circaea lutetiana Enchanter’s-nightshade Llysiau Steffan Flowering plant
Phyllobius pomaceus Green Nettle Weevil - Insect - beetle (Coleoptera)
Hirundo rustica Swallow Gwennol Bird
Alauda arvensis Skylark Ehedydd Bird
Quercus Oak Derwen Flowering plant
Pisaura mirabilis Tent Spider - spider (Araneae)
Rubus fruticosus agg. Bramble Llwyn Mwyar Duon Flowering plant
Allium ursinum Ramsons Craf Y Geifr Flowering plant
Ulex europaeus Gorse Eithinen Ffrengig Flowering plant
Blechnum spicant Hard-Fern Gwibredynen Fern
Andricus foecundatrix f. agamic Artichoke Gall Causer - Insect - hymenopteran
Clytocerus - Insect - true fly (Diptera)
Oniscus asellus Common Shiny Woodlouse Pryfed Coed (?) crustacean
Anthoxanthum odoratum Sweet Vernal-grass Perwellt Y Gwanwyn Flowering plant
Holcus lanatus Yorkshire-fog Maswellt Penwyn Flowering plant
Phylloscopus trochilus Willow Warbler Telor Yr Helyg Bird
Polystichum setiferum Soft Shield-Fern Gwrychredynen Feddal Fern
Fraxinus excelsior Ash Onnen Flowering plant
Cynips quercusfolii f. agamic Cherry Gall Causer - Insect - hymenopteran
Turdus merula BlackBird Mwyalch Bird
Dryopteris dilatata Broad Buckler-Fern Marchredynen Lydan Fern
Eupeodes corollae Insect - true fly (Diptera)
Juncus effusus Soft-rush Brwynen Babwyr Flowering plant
Hedera hibernica Atlantic Ivy Iorwg Yr Iwerydd Flowering plant
Corylus Hazel Collen Flowering plant
Vulpes vulpes Red Fox Cadno terrestrial mammal
Puccinia punctiformis Thistle Rust Rhwd Ysgall Fungus
Melampyrum pratense Common Cow-wheat Gliniogai Flowering plant
Vaccinium myrtillus Bilberry Llusen Flowering plant
Troglodytes troglodytes Wren Dryw Bird
Chrysosplenium oppositifolium Opposite-leaved Golden-saxifrage Eglyn Cyferbynddail Flowering plant
Fringilla coelebs Chaffinch Ji-binc Bird
Phyllitis scolopendrium Hart’s-tongue Tafod Yr Hydd Fern
Flavoparmelia caperata Lichen
Cirsium arvense Creeping Thistle Ysgallen Y Maes Flowering plant
Ficedula hypoleuca Pied Flycatcher Gwybedog Brith Bird
Taraxacum officinale agg. Dandelion Dant Y Llew Flowering plant
Calluna vulgaris Heather Grug Flowering plant
Ilex aquifolium Holly Celynnen Flowering plant
Hyacinthoides non-scripta Bluebell Clychau’r Gog Flowering plant
Anemone nemorosa Wood Anemone Blodyn Y Gwynt Flowering plant
Luzula sylvatica Great Wood-rush Coedfrwynen Fawr Flowering plant
Crataegus monogyna Hawthorn Draenen Wen Flowering plant
Cyanistes caeruleus Blue Tit Titw Tomos Las Bird
Fagus sylvatica Beech Ffawydden Flowering plant
Phylloscopus collybita Chiffchaff Siff-saff Bird
Urtica dioica Common Nettle Danhadlen Boeth Flowering plant
Talpa europaea European Mole Gwahadden terrestrial mammal
Parmotrema perlatum Lichen
Prunus spinosa Blackthorn Draenen Ddu Flowering plant

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi Workshop 3 — Birds of the Oak
item.description:
Guest post by project artist Pip Lewis

Adar y Dderw – birds of the Oak

Pip continued working with Group A, creating layered artworks made with acetate . The process involves overlaying drawing and collage, and adding text, and will be exhibited in the Stori Wyllt Gathering in Stiwdio 3 in Aberteifi on Monday 29th June.

Prior to the workshop Pip selected and printed A3 photos of different birds who live and forage in oak trees. The students drew lots last week to find out which bird they would be focusing on in their final artwork.

item.title: Stiwdio3 Café reccie for event
item.description:
This gives an overview of the upstairs gallery space.

Gallery Oriel

item.title: List of bird species and the Oak / Derwen
item.description:
This is the list of bird species that have relationship with the Oak / Derwen, that the artist Pip Lewis is working on with the Group A students.

Species English name Welsh name
Phoenicurus phoenicurus Redstart Tingoch
Ficedula hypoleuca Pied Flycatcher Gwybedog Brith
Corvus frugilegus Rook Ydfan
Parus major Great Tit Titw Mawr
Cyanistes caeruleus Blue Tit Titw Tomos Las
Anthus trivialis Tree Pipit Corhedydd Y Coed
Phylloscopus sibilatrix Wood Warbler Telor Y Coed
Columba palumbus Wood Pigeon Colomen Y Goedwig
Sturnus vulgaris Starling Drudwy
Athene noctua Little Owl Tylluan Fach
Garrulus glandarius Jay Sgrech Y Coed
Erithacus rubecula Robin Robin Goch
Dendrocopos major Great Spotted Woodpecker Cnocell Fraith Fwyaf
Coccothraustes coccothraustes Hawfinch Gylfrinbraff
Sitta europaea Nuthatch Delor Y Cnau


Collage of Robin Goch, work-in-progress

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi Workshop 2 — Shape of the Oak leaf
item.description: The class was split into two groups (Group A & Group B ) and Pip worked with Group A, with the students initially studying Oak leaves and making observational drawings drawing of them with pencils, identifying the midrib tip, shape and margins of the leaf. The students also worked with white charcoal pencils drawings of Oak leaves on black paper, and black charcoal pencils on white paper, and graphite sticks to create rubbings of the leaves.

item.title: List of plant species at Ysgol Llanilar
item.description:
Ecologist Sheena Duller very kindly made a list of plant species that are on the school grounds. The translations are from Y Bwiadur website.

Species English name Welsh name Area
Cerastium Mouse Ear Clust-y-Llygoden
Hypochaeris radicata Cat’s Ear Melynydd
Achillea millefolium Yarrow Milddail
Plantago lanceolata Ribwort Plantain Llyriad Yr Ais
Crepis Hawksbeard Gwalchlys
Cardamine pratensis Cuckoo Flower Blodyn Llefrith
Ranunculus bulbosus Bulbous Buttercup Blodyn-Ymenyn Bondew
Ranunculus arvensis Meadow Buttercup Blodyn Ymenyn
Ranuculus repens Creeping Buttercup Blodyn-Ymenyn Ymlusgol
Sagina Pearlwort Corwlyddyn Y Mynydd Between paving slabs
Plantago major Greater Plantain Llydan Y Ffordd
Hypochaeris radicata Cat’s Ear Melynydd
- Moss Mwsogl
Lichens Cennau
Veronica arvensis Wall Speedwell Rhwyddlwyn Y Fagwyr
Poa annua Annual Meadow Grass Gweunwellt Unflwydd
Grasses Car park lawn
Anthoxanthum odoratum Sweet Vernal Perwellt Y Gwanwyn
Alopecurus pratensis Meadow Foxtail Cynffonwellt Y Maes
Festuca rubra Red Fescue Peiswellt Coch
Poa annua Annual Meadow Grass Gweunwellt Unflwydd
Dactylis glomerata Cocksfoot Troed Y Ceiliog
Luzula campestris Field Wood Rush Milfyw
Holcus lanatus Yorkshire Fog Maswellt Penwyn
Flowers
Trifolium repens White Clover Meillionen Wen
Trifolium pratense Red Clover Meillionen Goch
Trifolium dubium Lesser Trefoil Meillionen Hopysaidd Fach
Lotus corniculatus Birds Foot Trefoil Pysen-Y-Ceirw
Vicia Vetch Llaethwyg
Veronica chamaedrys Germander Speedwell Llygad Doli
Veronica serpyllifolia Thyme-leaved Speedwell Rhwyddlwyn Dail Teim
Veronica officinalis Common Speedwell Rhwyddlwyn Meddygol
Geranium lucidum Shining Cranesbill Pig-Yr-Aran Loywddail
Bellis perennis Daisy Llygad Y Dydd
Taraxacum officinale Dandelion Dant Y Llew

item.title: Ysgol Llanilar Workshop 3 — Introducing cyanotype prints
item.description:
A cyanotype is a monochrome print made by placing objects on a pre-prepared light-sensitive surface†

What I have absolutely loved about the Stori Wyllt workshops so far is the range of activities and the different types of engagement (clay making, charcoal baking, bubble blowing, story telling, crayon rubbing & more).

At Ysgol Llanilar, I am entranced by making cyanotypes, the Prussian Blue tinted proto-photographic method of objects on light-sensitive paper, and artist Marian Haf has already helped the students create some truly gorgeous pieces.

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi workshop outline
item.description:
The sessions will provide opportunities to:

  • Explore and experiment with a range of art techniques including rubbing, observational drawing, stencilling, collage, wax resist, tracing, pattern-making
  • Learn about the birds, moths and butterflies associated with oak trees
  • Learn about patterns in nature
  • Create a set of observational drawings of leaves
  • Create a set of composite images of birds, moths and butterflies associated with oak trees

Group A - leaves and birds

Time Session 1
Workshop 2 - Thu 30 Apr AM		 Session 2
Workshop 3 - Thu 7 May AM
9.30‑10.30 Leaf rubbing
Observational drawing		 Collage warm up
Collaging bird image
Break
10.45‑11.50 Key birds living in oaks

Photo lottery
Sketch image
Copy onto acetate		 Stencilling bird names Welsh and English

Sharing circle
Group B with Jake Group B with Yusef

Group B - patterns in nature

Time Session 1
Workshop 4 - Thu 21 May AM		 Session 2
Workshop 5 - Thu 4 Jun PM
9.30‑10.30
(12.50‑2.10)		 Warm-up mark-making
Group excercise

Pattern
Ink pastel resist		 Applying patterns to image

Completing image
Break
10.45‑11.50
(2.25‑3.10)		 Key moths & butterflies living in oaks

Trace image onto tracing paper
Stencil name of moth/butterfly
Create pattern sheet		 Sharing circle
Group A with Jake Everyone Yusef Netpool Park AM
Group A with Jake PM

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi Workshop 1 — ID around the Oak
item.description:
Underlining the variation in the different schools, settings and people across Stori Wyllt, we didn’t have an ecologist for Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi for the first two workshops, as Yusef Samari has a very full calendar with the Your Wild Patch / Eich Darn Gwyllt project.

This meant I was leading the first session, very ably aided by teacher Mari Roberts (who has a Zoology degree!) I gave a very brief introduction to ecoystems, and talked a bit about the wildlife benefits of the Oak, and the amazing array and number of species it supports. And then out to the seating area under a pair of Oak trees, beyond the playground and playing field, newly garlanded with a shining green fence.

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron Workshop 2 — Storytelling at Allt y Graig
item.description:
We were still waiting for the go-ahead to the house of the witch Mari Berllan Peter in Esgair Arth woodland, so Plan B was put into operation, a trip to Allt y Graig woods, just the other side of Jewsons where the Twr Gwyllt (Wild Tower) is situated, with storyteller and artist Rebecca Wyn Kelly and ecologist Carys May.

The walk up Bro Allt yr Graig is steeply wooded banks, a single track road and swathes of plant life. At the beginning of the track in the woods we found a book. Under a saucepan. The book is called “A Scary Adventure” and it’s discovery was a strange and happenstance.

item.title: Ysgol Llanarth Workshop 2 — Forest school fun
item.description:
Jo Dainty is a forest school practitioner and she crammed an incredible amount into the two hours on the Ysgol Llanarth school field, in a very entertaining and high-octane workshop.

First of all, she set up a big circle with carpet mats for all the students, and had them sit down around the fire bowl. This is such a good tip, as it was a damp April day in a damp field. Jo had brought some lengths of willow with her, and there was a large patch of willow (Grey Willow, Salix atrocinerea?) at the bottom of the field as well.

item.title: Ysgol Llanilar Workshop 2 - Ecosystems & nature patterns
item.description:
For the second workshop at Ysgol Llanilar, the class of 23 was split into two groups of a dozen. One group went with Sheena the ecologist, and the other with Marian the artist. Sheena was also joined by mycologist and educationalist Louise Tranter

Sheena talked about ecosystems, the tree of life and then took the group outside, showing different habitats around the pond and the river.

The students with Marian were sketching the shapes and outlines of leaves, and then went outside to take photographs of patterns in nature. These photographs will be used alongside plants in the cyanotype printing process, which uses ferric ammonium citrate/oxalate and potassium ferricyanide and water to develop and fix.

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi and wildlife partnership work in Cardigan
item.description:
The Grassland ID workshops, organised by Ceredigion Nature Partnership in 2025 were a great success, and showed a fantastic working partnership between the Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi, Friends of Netpool Park, West Wales Biodiversity Information Centre and the Partnership.

What has inspired Stori Wyllt in addition to the great work at Netpool Park has been the collaboration between the school, Wales & West Housing Assocation and Cardished to create wildlife habitat with residents and students. WWHA received a Small Grant from the Partnership for wildlife enhancements across their Ceredigion properties, and this included the creation of bug hotels with the students from Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi and the residents of Llys Owen, with a workshop run by Harry from Cardished.

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron
item.description: Carys Griffiths-Jones is a key inspiration for Stori Wyllt, the head of Year 5 and co-lead of The Expressive Arts.

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi
item.description: Key Stage 2 teacher Mari Roberts has a passion for wildlife and outdoor learning, sharing lessons across classes.

item.title: Ysgol Llanarth
item.description: The students have a high attainment in wildlife ID, and teacher Cally Jones has been intrepid in secure in outdoor learning experiences.

item.title: Ysgol Llanilar
item.description: Headteacher Jannette Jones has led from the front in developing the school gardens and engaging with Stori Wyllt.

item.title: Am Stori Wyllt
item.description:
Dod â natur yn fyw. ID bywyd gwyllt, creu cynefinoedd a gweithdai adrodd straeon i greu Stori Wyllt. Bring nature to life. Wildlife ID, habitat creation & storytelling workshops to create a Wild Story.

Mae Stori Wyllt yn brosiect peilot ar gyfer ysgolion cynradd yng Cheredigion sy’n rhedeg o fis Mawrth i fis Mehefin 2026, gyda’r nod o ymgorffori adferiad natur ym mywyd yr ysgol, wedi‘i drefnu gan Bartneriaeth Natur Ceredigion. Stori Wyllt is a pilot project for primary schools in Ceredigion running from March to June 2026, aiming to embed nature recovery into school life, organised by Ceredigion Nature Partnership.

item.title: Ysgol Llanarth Workshop 1 — Worms & clay!
item.description:
NB: The notes before Easter have been written in retrospect, which means they lack detail. In future, I’ll be writing notes immediately afterwards, and also asking for immediate & short feedback from students and participants.

This workshop was a riot, in the best sense of the word! I made a quick introduction about Stori Wyllt but I was a bit worried about the rain, so we headed off to Molly‘s field Cae Ysgubor. The route is via a public bridleway by the side of the nursery and campsite. However, it was very wet, with some localised flooding! The path also turned muddy going uphill, with some surface water. But everyone seemed very positive.

item.title: Image workflow
item.description:
1. Take photos, make notes
  2. Choose, edit, crop & upload to Signal group
  3. Download files to PC
  4. Rename with timestamp & description eg 250320-aberaeron-workshop1-toad.jpg
  5. Upload to Cloudinary Stori Wyllt 2026 folder
  6. Copy URL:
  7. Paste into image frontmatter property
  8. Or paste inline into body of document: ![AltText](URL) Which looks like this:
![Toad home pile of stones and wood and moss](https://res.cloudinary.com/naturceredigion/image/upload/v1774433498/260320-lletau-llyffant-root-moss-stone-twr-gwyllt-169.jpg)

Straight image

Figure image

Gallery

Gallery with figure & figcaption

item.title: Ysgol Llanilar Workshop 1 — Lichen & buds
item.description:
NB: The notes before Easter have been written in retrospect, which means they lack detail. In future, I’ll be writing notes immediately afterwards, and also asking for immediate & short feedback from students and participants.

The workshop started with a fantastic session in the classroom with ecologist Sheena Duller, who had brought in a veritable cornucopia of lichens, native plants and tree buds and flowers. The students seemed really enamoured of the lichen on the branches (with a couple of examples on rocks), particularly when using the hand lenses. The sheer diversity of species really hit home.

item.title: Mishaps & magic at Esgair Arth
item.description:
After the first workshop for Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron in the morning, I returned back up to a potential site just north of Aberaeron and inland from Aberarth, on the grounds of a farm called Esgair Arth. It is the old house of a famous local witch called Mari Berllan Peter, about whom our artist Rebecca Wyn Kelly has done so much work already.


Mari Berllan Peter‘s old house by the Afon Arth

item.title: Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron Workshop 1 — Jewson wildlife garden
item.description:
NB: The notes before Easter have been written in retrospect, which means they lack detail. In future, I’ll be writing notes immediately afterwards, and also asking for immediate & short feedback from students and participants.

Ecologist Carys started in the classroom with an exercise around food webs and different living things (plants, animals, insects, fungi etc), and was drawing suggestions from the class and discussing the relationships between them.

We walked along the south bank of the Afon Aeron, past the allotments, and to the small garden south-west facing garden at Jewsons where the separate project Tŵr Gwyllt is being built by artist Rebecca Wyn Kelly. When we arrived, Rebecca was at the controls of a mini-digger, prepping the site!

item.title: Tŵr Gwyllt at Aberaeron
item.description:
Guest post by artist Rebecca Wyn Kelly:

While Jake was visiting the Jewsons yard with Ysgol Aberaeron, we came across this toad tucked beneath a log, a quiet little resident already making use of the space.

I’m really hoping he might take shelter in one of the Lletau Llyffant I’ve created; these are small shelters made with the toads in mind.

A little about Llyffantod

In old Welsh folk belief, toads were linked to wise women and hidden knowledge, and were said to carry carreg y llyffant in their heads, a magical stone with protective powers. Living between water and land, tucked beneath logs and stones, they’re natural threshold keepers, creatures of the in-between, brushing right up against the Otherworld of Annwn.

item.title: Aberaeron site reccie
item.description:
Last year, we ran the Grassland ID workshops at Penmorfa with Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron.

This year, we’re hoping for another magical spot, with a witch’s house, but this has yet to be confirmed. I had a chance meeting with my colleague, Tree Officer Norman Birch, and he explained that there is a public foot path in Aberaeron that runs on the south bank of the river Aeron, past the allotments an up to Jewsons.

item.title: Ysgol Llanilar site visit
item.description:
A foundational element of the Stori Wyllt project has been to foster strong relationships between the schools, creatives and naturalists. This is why the initial Stori Wyllt Symposiwm was so important; an opportunity for practitioners to meet each other and ask questions.

On a gloriously sunny Thursday afternoon, I met up with ecologist Sheena Duller, artist Marian Haf and headteacher at Ysgol Llanarth, Jannette Jones.

We walked first past the village pond

item.title: Stori Wyllt Symposiwm
item.description:
Croeso i’r Stori Wyllt symposiwm!
Welcome to the Stori Wyllt symposium!

The Stori Wyllt Symposiwm was an ideal opportunity for the teachers, naturalists and creatives from the four schools to meet each other in-person and share ideas at a central location in Ceredigion (Moody Meadows at Bargoed Farm ar bwys Llwyncelyn).

The Stori Wyllt schools are, from the south to the north, Ysgol Gynradd Aberteifi, Ysgol Llanarth, Ysgol Gynradd Aberaeron and Ysgol Llanilar.

item.title: Workshops
item.description:
Sessions

  1. Introduction to Plants and Insects - naturalist
    • Objective: Learn basics of plant and animal identification
  2. Food webs, habitat, observation - naturalist & creative
    • Objective 1: Gain understanding of food web and ecological roles of plants and insects
    • Objective 2: Build on skills of observation and description for plants (visual and written)
  3. Insect id skills and recording - naturalist & creative
    • Objective 1: Further ID skills linking recording and habitat
    • Objective 2: Skills of observation and description for insects (visual and written)
  4. Creatives

Session 1. Introduction to Plants and Insects

Objective: Learn basics of plant and animal identification

item.title: Elements
item.description:
• Llyfr Natur
  • Field Studies Council leaflets
  • Plantlife Cymru
  • Botanic Garden Wales education resources
